hero.headline: How does Maine termination law and at-will exceptions work?
hero.subhead: At-will with a payout most operators miss: Maine's Earned Paid Leave statute requires unused-leave payment on separation, not just a final paycheck by the next payday.

answer_capsule: Maine is an at-will state, but the Earned Paid Leave statute (26 M.R.S. §637) creates a termination liability most operators miss: employers with 10 or more employees must pay out accrued, unused EPL at separation. Final wages follow the next regular payday or within 2 weeks of written demand under 26 M.R.S. §626. The Maine Human Rights Act (5 M.R.S. §4572) covers employers with 15 or more employees for most discrimination claims, with a 300-day EEOC charge window.

is_at_will.question: Is Maine an at-will employment state?
is_at_will.answer_first: Yes. Maine is an at-will state under common law. Either party can end employment at any time, for any lawful reason — or no reason at all.
is_at_will.body: Maine has no single statute that codifies the at-will doctrine. It rests on common law, shaped by decades of Maine Supreme Judicial Court decisions. Three recognised exception categories apply: public-policy, implied contract, and the Maine Human Rights Act.

final_pay.question: When must you pay a terminated employee in Maine?
final_pay.answer_first: Under 26 M.R.S. §626, final wages are due on the next regular payday for the period in which termination occurred. The 2-week window opens only after the employee makes a written demand.

pay_table.deadline: Next regular payday; or within 2 weeks of written demand

epl_payout.question: Does Maine require payout of Earned Paid Leave on termination?
epl_payout.answer_first: Yes, for employers with 10 or more employees. 26 M.R.S. §637 requires unused, accrued EPL to be paid out at separation. This obligation exists regardless of your written policy.

at_will_exceptions.question: What are the exceptions to at-will employment in Maine?
at_will_exceptions.answer_first: Maine recognises three exception categories: public-policy, implied contract, and statutory protections under the Maine Human Rights Act. The list is not exhaustive.

mhra.question: How does a Maine discrimination complaint work under the MHRA?
mhra.answer_first: Maine is a federal deferral state. An EEOC charge can be filed within 300 days of the alleged act. The Maine Human Rights Commission processes state-level complaints on a parallel track.

pfml.question: How does Maine's Paid Family and Medical Leave interact with termination?
pfml.answer_first: Maine PFML benefits started 1st May 2026. Retaliation for taking PFML leave is unlawful from that date, and PFML leave counts separately from EPL accrual.

good_to_know.question: What else should you know about terminating a Maine employee?
good_to_know.answer_first: Maine's minimum wage of $14.15/hour sets the floor for final-pay calculations. Handbook review matters more in Maine than in most at-will states because of the implied-contract case law.

faq_schema.q: Is Maine an at-will employment state?
faq_schema.a: Yes. Maine is an at-will employment state under common law. Either party can end the employment relationship at any time, for any reason not barred by statute, contract, or a recognised public-policy exception.

faq_schema.q: When must you pay a terminated employee in Maine?
faq_schema.a: Under 26 M.R.S. §626, wages earned are due on the next regular payday for the pay period in which separation occurred. If the employee makes a written demand, the employer has 2 weeks from that demand to pay.

faq_schema.q: Does Maine require payout of accrued Earned Paid Leave on termination?
faq_schema.a: Yes, for employers with 10 or more employees. Maine's Earned Paid Leave law (26 M.R.S. §637) requires employers to pay out any accrued, unused EPL balance at separation. Employees accrue 1 hour of EPL for every 40 hours worked, up to 40 hours per year.

faq_schema.q: What are the exceptions to at-will employment in Maine?
faq_schema.a: Maine recognises three main exceptions: public-policy (retaliation for workers' compensation claims, MHRA-protected activity, jury service), implied contract (handbook or offer-letter terms), and the Maine Human Rights Act covering employers with 15 or more employees for most claims.

faq_schema.q: How long does a Maine employee have to file a discrimination claim?
faq_schema.a: Maine is a federal deferral state: an EEOC charge can be filed within 300 days of the alleged act. Under the Maine Human Rights Act, claimants can also pursue a direct state-court action within 6 years.

meta.description: Maine at-will: EPL unused-leave payout on termination under 26 M.R.S. §637, final-pay demand trigger under §626, MHRA 300-day charge window, and workers' comp retaliation bar.
